title: vittadinia cuneata var. hirsuta
intro: Vittadinia cuneata var. hirsuta, also known as hairy New South Wales daisy or rough leafed daisy, is a variety of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to eastern Australia and is a shrub or herb with hairy leaves and white or yellow flowers. Vittadinia cuneata var. hirsuta has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation in the body.
